merger_description:
Winning Appliances is a retailer of kitchen, laundry and other home appliances which it supplies to consumers and businesses across Australia.

Winning Appliances proposes to establish a new holding company Winning Holdings and undertake the following steps:

1. the sale of all shares in Winning Appliances by the existing Winning Appliances shareholders to Winning Holdings, and
2. the issuing of shares in Winning Holdings to the previous shareholders of Winning Appliances, in equal proportions to their shareholdings in Winning Appliances (together,the **Acquisition**).

The share classes and rights of Winning Holdings following the Acquisition will reflect the share classes and rights of the existing shares in Winning Appliances.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Winning Group Holdings Pty Ltd (Winning Holdings) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of:
• Winning Holding’s acquisition of all shares in Winning Appliances
Pty Ltd (Winning Appliances) from the existing Winning
Appliances shareholders (the Shareholders), and
• the Shareholders’ acquisition, in equal proportions to their former
shareholdings in Winning Appliances, of all shares in Winning
Holdings,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

Winning Appliances supplies and installs kitchen, laundry and other
home appliances including household whitegoods, homewares and
audio-visual products, to consumers and businesses. Winning
Appliances also provides delivery, installation, warehousing and
commercial supply services to builders and developers through its
subsidiaries.
Winning Holdings is a newly established holding company that was
incorporated for the purposes of the Acquisition. It does not
currently provide, nor does it own or have an interest in any business
that provides, goods or services. As such, there is no competitive
overlap between the parties.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal
consolidation, removal of a competitor, or change to Winning
Appliances’ competitive position as a result of the Acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
The ACCC also considered the potential application of section
51ABD of the Act. The parties submit that there are strong grounds
that section 51ABD applies to the Acquisition, however, on the facts
of this matter, including the staged structure of the Acquisition, there
is some uncertainty about whether the Acquisition falls within the
scope of that provision. Having regard to the particular
circumstances, including the consequences of non-notification in
reliance on the exemption if it was found not to apply, the ACCC
considered it appropriate to make a determination on the basis that
the acquisition provisions in the Act apply to the Acquisition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
